page: Microsoft Azure D32a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DAv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7452 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.35 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 819 GB, while GPU acceleration is absent.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and PassMark CPU encryption tests scoring in the top 25%. Multi-core CPU performance, static web serving throughput, and Redis SET operations fall within the average range. Notably, the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 10%, reflecting high-tier memory subsystem efficiency, although large-block memory bandwidth read and write speeds remain average. For machine learning workloads, LLM text generation using a small 135M model achieves top-tier performance at 323.62 tokens per second, whereas prompt processing on a large 70B model is weak, scoring in the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32a_v4 offers a balanced resource profile, combining a high vCPU count with a substantial memory allocation and local storage without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. This makes it highly suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads, multi-threaded application servers, relational databases, web serving, and moderate software compilation tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure D32a_v4 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs, 16 physical cores, and a 3.35 GHz clock speed. It features 128.0 GB of RAM and 819 GB of local storage, operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier PassMark memory scores, alongside average multi-core and database execution capabilities. While it lacks GPU acceleration and exhibits weak performance on large-scale LLM inference, it delivers top-tier text generation speeds for smaller models. This instance provides a balanced resource density, making it a cost-effective option for enterprise web serving, relational databases, and multi-threaded application hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32a_v4 is a general-purpose VM featuring 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core CPU and top-tier memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32a_v4 is a general-purpose VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and 819 GB local storage, optimized for balanced workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, high-tier memory performance, and integrated local storage.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7452 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB RAM.
Includes 819 GB of local storage for data-intensive applications.
Delivers top-tier PassMark memory performance and strong single-core CPU execution.
Achieves high-tier text generation speeds for small-scale language models.
Best suited for relational databases, web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
